code_dossier.name: DeleteUserRequest.java
code_dossier.description: A Data Transfer Object (DTO) used to encapsulate user deletion request parameters, supporting batch deletion of users by passing an array of user IDs.

data.detailed_description:
DeleteUserRequest is a simple DTO (Data Transfer Object) class located in the account-api module's dto package. It serves as a request model for user deletion operations in the account management system.

The class leverages Lombok's @Data annotation to automatically generate boilerplate code including getters, setters, toString(), equals(), hashCode(), and a default constructor. This significantly reduces code verbosity while maintaining full functionality.

The single field `userId` is defined as a Long array (Long[]), indicating this DTO is designed to support batch user deletion operations. This design allows clients to request deletion of multiple users in a single API call, which is more efficient than making individual requests for each user.

As part of the account-api module, this DTO likely serves as a contract between the API layer and its consumers (frontend applications, other microservices, etc.). It defines the expected structure for delete user requests in a type-safe manner.

The placement in the `dto` package follows conventional Java project organization, clearly indicating its role as a data transfer object rather than a domain entity or service component.
responsibilities: Encapsulate user deletion request data for API communication
Support batch deletion by accepting an array of user IDs
Provide type-safe data transfer between API consumers and the account service
Define the contract/schema for user deletion API endpoints

interfaces.name: DeleteUserRequest
interfaces.description: DTO class for encapsulating batch user deletion requests with auto-generated accessor methods via Lombok @Data annotation
